name: Salna - Hotel style
ingredients: [{"id": 149, "name": "roasted gram", "quantity": 50, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 127, "name": "peanuts", "quantity": 50, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 50, "name": "coconut", "quantity": 40, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 31, "name": "cardamom", "quantity": 2, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 168, "name": "star anise", "quantity": 1, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 48, "name": "cinnamon", "quantity": 2, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 95, "name": "kalpasi", "quantity": 1, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 49, "name": "cloves", "quantity": 6, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 66, "name": "fennel seeds", "quantity": 0.5, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 170, "name": "sunflower oil", "quantity": 150, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 13, "name": "Bay leaves", "quantity": 2, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 121, "name": "onion", "quantity": 200, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 78, "name": "ginger garlic paste", "quantity": 15, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 84, "name": "green chilies", "quantity": 6, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 183, "name": "turmeric powder", "quantity": 0.5, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 47, "name": "chilli powder", "quantity": 2, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 223, "name": "cumin powder", "quantity": 0.5, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 54, "name": "coriander powder", "quantity": 1, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 74, "name": "garam masala", "quantity": 1, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 155, "name": "salad tomatoes", "quantity": 5, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 158, "name": "salt", "quantity": 2, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 53, "name": "coriander leaves", "quantity": 50, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 111, "name": "mint leaves", "quantity": 50, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 26, "name": "Brussels sprouts", "quantity": 3000, "serving_unit": "g"}]
instructions:
* Prepare the Paste:

   * Combine roasted gram (pottukadalai), peanuts, coconut, cardamom, star anise, a small piece of cinnamon, kalpasi, two cloves, and a little fennel seeds in a blender.

   * Grind these ingredients into a smooth paste.

 * Sauté Aromatics:

   * Heat 150 ml of oil in a large pot.

   * Add two bay leaves, two cloves, two small pieces of cinnamon, a small amount of kalpasi, a small piece of star anise, and four cloves to the hot oil.

   * Add 200 grams of sliced onions and sauté until golden brown.

   * Add one spoon of garlic paste and one spoon of ginger paste, then mix well.

   * Add six green chilies and continue to sauté.

 * Add Spices and Tomatoes:

   * Add half a spoon of turmeric powder, two spoons of chili powder, two spoons of cumin powder, one spoon of coriander powder, two spoons of mutton masala, and one spoon of garam masala.

   * Mix the spices thoroughly with the sautéed ingredients.

   * Add five chopped tomatoes and mix until they soften.

   * Add a little water and salt to taste.

   * Add a small bunch of coriander leaves and a slightly larger amount of mint leaves.

   * Continue to cook until the tomatoes are well mashed and the oil separates.

 * Incorporate the Ground Paste and Finish:

   * Add the previously ground paste to the pot and mix it in.

   * Pour in about 3 liters of water.

   * Check the salt level and adjust if needed.

   * Cover the pot and let it boil for about 10 minutes.

   * Once cooked, garnish with coriander leaves.

instructions:
1. Boil the sweet potatoes until tender, then mash with milk and oil/butter. 
2. Sauté onion, carrots, and celery until softened. Add garlic. 
3. Stir in the rinsed lentils, chopped tomatoes, vegetable stock, herbs, and soy sauce. Simmer for 30-40 minutes until lentils are tender. 
4. Preheat oven to 200°C. 
5. Spoon the lentil mixture into an ovenproof dish and top with the mashed sweet potato. 
6. Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden brown. Serve with steamed green vegetables.

name: egg fried rice
instructions: 1. Heat the vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. 2. Add the cooked rice and stir-fry for a few minutes. 3. Push the rice to one side of the pan and crack the eggs into the other side. Scramble the eggs and mix with the rice. 4. Add the soy sauce and chopped green onions. Stir until well combined and heated through. 5. Serve hot.

name: chicken fried rice
instructions:
1. Heat vegetable oil in a large pan over medium heat.
2. Add chicken and cook until browned and cooked through. Remove and set aside.
3. In the same pan, scramble the eggs until fully cooked. Add the fried rice to the pan. 
4. Stir in the chicken, soy sauce, and chopped green onions. Cook for a few more minutes until everything is well combined and heated through.
5. Serve hot.

name: English Quiche
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F). Roll out the pastry and line a tart tin. In a bowl, beat together the eggs, milk, double cream, salt, and pepper. Add cheese and pour the mixture into the pastry. Bake for 25-30 minutes until set and golden. Allow to cool slightly before serving.

name: Beef and Onion Pie
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). In a pan, brown the ground beef and onions until cooked. Add beef stock, salt, and pepper; simmer for 10 minutes. Line a pie dish with pastry, fill with the beef mixture, and cover with another pastry layer. Bake for 25-30 minutes or until golden brown. Allow to cool slightly before serving.

name: Pancake with Maple Syrup
instructions: 1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the wheat flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt. 2. In another bowl, whisk together the milk, eggs, and melted butter. 3. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. 4. Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and pour 1/4 cup of the batter for each pancake. 5. Cook until bubbles form on the surface, then flip and cook until golden brown. 6. Serve warm with maple syrup drizzled on top.

name: Grilled Cheese Sandwich
instructions: 1. Heat a skillet over medium heat. 2. Spread butter on one side of each slice of bread. 3. Place one slice of bread, butter side down, in the skillet. 4. Add the American cheese on top of the bread. 5. Place the second slice of bread on top, butter side up. 6. Grill until the bread is golden brown and the cheese is melted, about 3-4 minutes per side. 7. Remove from heat, slice in half, and serve warm.

Roll up the burritos, fold in the sides, and serve warm.

name: Cobb Salad
ingredients: [{"id": 90, "name": "head romaine lettuce", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 252, "name": "grape tomatoes", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 254, "name": "hard-boiled eggs", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 245, "name": "blue cheese dressing", "quantity": 50.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 8, "name": "avocado", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 253, "name": "bacon", "quantity": 50.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 17, "name": "black beans", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 255, "name": "red wine vinegar", "quantity": 10.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 119, "name": "olive oil", "quantity": 20.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 27, "name": "butter", "quantity": 10.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 196, "name": "cooked chicken breast", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}]
instructions: 1. Wash and chop the romaine lettuce and place it in a bowl. 2. Halve the grape tomatoes and add them to the bowl. 3. Add diced cooked chicken breast, crumbled bacon, chopped hard-boiled eggs, diced avocado, and blue cheese dressing on top. 4. Drizzle with olive oil and red wine vinegar, then toss gently to combine. 5. Serve immediately.

name: Chocolate Chip Cookies
ingredients: [{"id": 260, "name": "all-purpose flour", "quantity": 300.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 259, "name": "baking soda", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 158, "name": "salt", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 258, "name": "unsalted butter", "quantity": 170.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 257, "name": "chocolate chips", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 256, "name": "brown sugar", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 19, "name": "black salt", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 240, "name": "eggs", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 16, "name": "granulated sugar", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 18, "name": "vanilla extract", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}]
instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). 2. In a bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt. 3. In another bowl, cream together butter, granulated sugar, brown sugar, and vanilla extract. 4. Beat in eggs one at a time. 5. Gradually blend in the dry mix. 6. Stir in chocolate chips. 7. Drop spoonfuls onto a baking sheet. 8. Bake for 10-12 minutes or until golden brown. 9. Allow cooling before serving.

name: Mac and Cheese
instructions: 1. Cook the elbow macaroni according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside. 2. In a saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Stir in flour and cook for a minute. 3. Gradually whisk in milk and cook until the sauce thickens. 4. Add in cheddar cheese, salt, and pepper. Stir until smooth. 5. Combine the cheese sauce with the macaroni and mix well. 6. Pour into a baking dish and top with breadcrumbs. 7. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 20-25 minutes until golden brown. 8. Serve warm.

name: Avocado Toast
instructions: 1. Toast the slices of bread until golden brown. 2. In a bowl, mash the avocado with lemon juice, salt, and pepper. 3. Spread the mashed avocado evenly on each slice of toast. 4. Drizzle with olive oil. 5. Serve immediately.

name: meat pie
instructions: Preheat your oven to 200°C (390°F). In a large pan, cook 500g of ground beef with chopped onion (1) and minced garlic (2 tsp) until browned. Add 1 cup of beef stock, and season with 1 tsp salt and 0.5 tsp black pepper. Simmer for about 10 minutes. Roll out puff pastry, line a pie dish, and fill it with the beef mixture. Cover with another pastry layer, seal the edges, and make a slit on top. Beat 1 egg and brush over the surface. Bake for 30-35 minutes until golden brown.

name: Lamingtons
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 180°C (355°F). Grease and line a rectangular cake tin. 
2. In a bowl, beat together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition. 
3. Sift in the self-raising flour and cocoa powder, then gently fold in the milk and water until combined. 
4. Pour the batter into the prepared tin and smooth the surface. Bake for 25 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean. 
5. Allow the cake to cool completely before cutting into squares. 
6. Dip each square in melted chocolate, then roll in desiccated coconut to coat. 
7. Allow to set before serving.

name: Grilled Barramundi
instructions:
1. Preheat the grill on medium-high heat.
2. Brush the Barramundi fillets with olive oil and season with salt and black pepper.
3. Place the fillets on the grill, skin-side down, and cook for about 4-5 minutes.
4. Flip the fillets carefully and cook for another 4-5 minutes, or until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
5. In the last minute of grilling, add wood chips for smoke flavor (if desired).
6. Remove the fish from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes.
7. Serve with lemon wedges and enjoy!

name: Vegemite on Toast
instructions:
1. Toast the slices of bread until golden brown. 
2. Spread a thin layer of Vegemite on the toast. 
3. Serve immediately and enjoy your Vegemite on toast!

name: Kangaroo Steak
instructions: 1. Preheat your grill to high heat. 2. Season the kangaroo steak with salt and pepper. 3. Place the steak on the grill for about 4-5 minutes per side, depending on thickness. 4. Remove from the grill and let rest for a few minutes. 5. Slice and serve with your choice of sides.

name: Scones with Clotted Cream
instructions: 1. Preheat your oven to 220°C (425°F). 2. In a bowl, mix the flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt. 3. Cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. 4. Add milk or cream gradually to form a soft dough. 5. Turn onto a floured surface and knead gently. 6. Roll out to about 2 cm thick and cut into rounds. 7. Place on a baking sheet and bake for 10-12 minutes until golden. 8. Serve warm with clotted cream.

name: Fish and Chips
instructions:
1. Peel and cut the potatoes into thick chips, then soak in cold water to remove excess starch.
2. In a bowl, mix flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper.
3. Heat oil in a deep fryer or large pot.
4. Dry the potatoes and fry them until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
5. Dredge the fish in the flour mixture, then carefully place it in the hot oil. Fry until golden and cooked through.
6. Serve the fried fish with chips on the side, drizzled with apple cider vinegar to taste.

name: Porridge
instructions: 1. In a pot, combine the porridge oats with water and milk. 2. Add salt and stir well. 3. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. 4. Once it thickens to your desired consistency, remove it from heat. 5. Serve topped with your choice of fruit toppings.

name: Jerk Chicken
instructions: 1. Preheat your grill to medium heat. 2. Rub the jerk seasoning all over the chicken pieces, making sure to cover them evenly. 3. Let the chicken marinate for at least 30 minutes, preferably overnight in the fridge. 4. Place the chicken on the grill and cook for about 30-40 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and has nice grill marks. 5. Serve hot with your choice of sides.

name: Brown Stew Fish
instructions:
1. Clean the fish and cut it into pieces.
2. Marinate the fish with lime juice, salt, and black pepper for about 30 minutes.
3. Heat the oil in a pan and fry the onions, garlic, and bell pepper until soft.
4. Add the tomatoes and thyme, cooking until the tomatoes are mushy.
5. Add the fish to the pan and cook on low heat until the fish is cooked through, about 10-15 minutes.
6. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Johnny Cakes
instructions: 1. In a bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar. Mix well. 2. Gradually add water while stirring to form a thick batter. 3. Fold in melted butter, green onion, and cheese. 4. Heat oil in a frying pan over medium heat. 5. Scoop batter onto the hot pan, forming small cakes. 6. Cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side until golden brown. 7. Remove and drain on paper towels. Serve warm.

name: Curry Goat
ingredients: [{"id": 295, "name": "goat meat", "quantity": 1000.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 296, "name": "curry powder", "quantity": 50.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 72, "name": "fried onions", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 121, "name": "onion", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 75, "name": "garlic", "quantity": 4.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 77, "name": "ginger", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 175, "name": "thyme", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 19, "name": "black salt", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 18, "name": "black pepper", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 51, "name": "coconut oil", "quantity": 3.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 188, "name": "water", "quantity": 4.0, "serving_unit": "cup"}]
instructions: 1. In a pot, heat the coconut oil over medium heat. 2. Add the fried onions and sauté until they are golden brown. 3. Add the garlic, ginger, and thyme, stirring for about 1 minute. 4. Add the goat meat to the pot and brown on all sides. 5. Sprinkle curry powder, black salt, and black pepper over the meat, mixing well to coat. 6. Pour in water and bring to a boil. 7. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours or until the goat meat is tender. 8. In the last 15 minutes of cooking, add the scotch bonnet pepper (whole or chopped, depending on desired heat). 9. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Congee with Century Egg
instructions: 1. Rinse the congee rice until the water runs clear. 2. In a pot, combine the rice with water (about 800ml). 3. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. 4. Cook for about 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a porridge-like consistency. 5. While it's cooking, prepare the century eggs by peeling and cutting them into quarters. 6. Once the congee is ready, serve it warm, topped with century egg pieces and any additional toppings as desired.

name: Scallion Pancakes
instructions: 1. In a large bowl, combine the flour and salt. 2. Gradually add water and mix until a soft dough forms. 3. Knead the dough for about 5 minutes until smooth. 4. Cover the dough with a towel and let it rest for 30 minutes. 5. After resting, divide the dough into equal portions. 6. Roll out each portion into a thin circle. 7. Brush with olive oil and sprinkle with sliced scallions. 8. Roll the dough tightly into a log, then coil it into a spiral. 9. Flatten the spiral gently, then roll out again into a thin pancake. 10. Heat oil in a skillet and cook each pancake until golden brown on both sides, about 2-3 minutes each. 11. Serve hot with dipping sauce.

name: Sesame Balls (Jian Dui)
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, combine glutinous rice flour, sugar, and water to form a dough. 2. Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a golf ball. 3. Flatten each ball slightly and place a small amount of sweet filling (like red bean paste) in the center, then mold the dough around the filling and shape it into a ball again. 4. Roll the balls in sesame seeds until well coated. 5. Heat vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat. 6. Once the oil is hot, carefully drop the sesame balls into the oil. Fry until they puff up and turn golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. 7. Remove from oil and drain on paper towels. 8. Serve warm as a snack or dessert.

name: Nyama Choma
instructions: 1. Cut the beef into chunks and marinate with salt, black pepper, and vegetable oil for at least an hour. 2. Preheat a grill to medium-high heat. 3. Cook the marinated beef on the grill, turning occasionally until well browned on all sides, about 10-15 minutes. 4. While the beef is grilling, sauté the sukuma wiki in a pan with a bit of vegetable oil until wilted. 5. Serve the grilled beef with the sautéed sukuma wiki on the side. Enjoy your delicious Nyama Choma!

name: Samosa
instructions: 1. In a pot, boil peeled potatoes until soft, then mash them. 2. In a pan, heat oil, add onions, and sauté until golden. 3. Add peas and spice mixture, and cook for a few minutes. Combine with mashed potatoes. 4. Roll out the dough and cut into circles. Place filling in the center and fold to form a triangle. 5. Heat oil for frying, and fry samosas until golden brown. 6. Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

name: Ethiopian Doro Wat
instructions: 1. Heat butter in a pan, and sauté onions until translucent. 2. Add ginger and garlic, and cook for another 2 minutes. 3. Stir in the berbere spice and add the chicken pieces, coating them well. 4. Add water, cover, and let simmer for about 30 minutes. 5. Stir occasionally until chicken is fully cooked. 6. Add boiled eggs towards the end and serve with injera or rice.

name: Coconut Rice and Beans
instructions:
1. Heat vegetable oil in a pot over medium heat. 
2. Add chopped onion, garlic, and ginger, and sauté until the onion is soft. 
3. Add coconut rice, coconut milk, salt, and pepper. Stir well. 
4. Add baked beans and mix thoroughly. 
5. Pour in water according to rice package instructions. 
6. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer until rice is cooked. 
7. Fluff the rice with a fork and serve warm.

name: Chapati
instructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine the chapati flour and salt. 
2. Gradually add water while mixing with your hand to form a soft dough. 
3. Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for at least 30 minutes. 
4. Divide the dough into equal portions and roll each portion into a ball. 
5. On a floured surface, roll out each ball into a thin circle. 
6. Heat oil in a frying pan over medium heat. 
7. Place the rolled-out chapati in the pan and cook until bubbles form. Flip and cook the other side until brown spots appear. 
8. Remove and brush with oil, if desired. 
9. Serve warm with your favorite dish.

name: Zanzibar Fish Curry
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add onions and sauté until translucent. 2. Stir in garlic and ginger, cooking for another minute. 3. Add tomatoes, coconut milk, curry powder, and salt. Let it simmer for 10 minutes. 4. Add the fish pieces and cook gently until the fish is flaky and cooked through. 5. Stir in chopped coriander before serving. Serve with rice or bread.

name: Croissant
instructions: 1. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). 2. Place the croissant on a baking sheet. 3. Bake in the oven for about 5-7 minutes until heated through and slightly crispy. 4. Serve warm, optionally with butter or jam.

name: Omelette au Fromage
instructions: 1. In a bowl, whisk together the eggs, salt, and black pepper. 2. Heat butter in a non-stick pan over medium heat until melted. 3. Pour the egg mixture into the pan, tilting the pan to ensure even coverage. 4. Cook until the edges begin to set, then sprinkle cheese on one half. 5. Fold the omelette in half and cook for an additional minute until the cheese is melted. 6. Slide the omelette onto a plate and serve warm.

name: Greek Yogurt with Honey
instructions:
1. In a bowl, place the Greek yogurt as the base.
2. Drizzle honey over the yogurt to add sweetness.
3. Top with a choice of nuts for crunch and texture.
4. Add fresh fruits for added flavor and color.
5. Mix everything together and enjoy your delicious breakfast!

name: Spaghetti Carbonara
instructions: 1. Cook spaghetti in a large pot of salted boiling water until al dente. 2. In a bowl, beat the eggs, and mix in 100g of Pecorino cheese and black pepper. 3. In a pan, cook the guanciale until crispy, then remove from heat. 4. Once spaghetti is cooked, reserve a cup of pasta water, then drain and place spaghetti in the pan over low heat. 5. Add the egg mixture quickly, mixing to create a creamy sauce. If too thick, add reserved pasta water to achieve desired consistency. 6. Serve with extra Pecorino cheese and freshly grated black pepper on top.

name: Frittata
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, salt, and pepper.
3. Heat a skillet over medium heat and sauté chopped onions and spinach until soft.
4. Pour the egg mixture over the vegetables in the skillet, and cook until the edges begin to set.
5. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, and transfer the skillet to the oven.
6. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the frittata is firm and golden.
7. Remove from oven, let cool slightly, and cut into wedges to serve.

name: Caprese Salad
instructions: 1. Slice the tomatoes and mozzarella cheese into thin rounds. 2. Arrange the tomato slices on a plate, alternating with the mozzarella slices. 3. Tuck fresh basil leaves between the layers. 4. Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic vinegar. 5. Season with salt and pepper to taste. 6. Serve immediately at room temperature.

name: Arancini
instructions: 1. Cook Arborio rice and let it cool. 2. Prepare a filling of cheese and marinara sauce. 3. Take a small amount of rice, flatten it, add the filling, and close it to form a ball. 4. Roll the balls in breadcrumbs. 5. Deep fry the arancini until golden brown.

name: Osso Buco
instructions: 1. Season the osso buco with salt and pepper. 2. Heat butter in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. 3. Brown the osso buco on all sides. 4. Remove the meat and add onion, carrot, celery, and garlic. 5. Sauté until soft. 6. Stir in tomato paste and cook for a couple of minutes. 7. Pour in the dry white wine and scrape up any brown bits. 8. Add broth, bay leaf, and rosemary. 9. Return the osso buco to the pot. 10. Cover and simmer for 1.5 hours or until tender.

name: Kimchi Fried Rice
instructions: 1. Heat sesame oil in a pan over medium heat. 2. Add the kimchi and stir-fry for a couple of minutes. 3. Add the cooked rice and mix well. 4. Pour in the soy sauce and stir continuously for about 2-3 minutes. 5. Push the rice to one side of the pan and crack the egg into the empty side, scrambling it gently. 6. Once the egg is cooked, mix it with the rice. 7. Garnish with chopped green onions before serving.

name: Bibimbap
instructions: 1. Cook the rice according to package instructions. 2. Stir-fry the mixed vegetables until tender. 3. Cook the meat in a pan until browned. 4. Fry an egg sunny-side up. 5. In a bowl, layer rice, vegetables, and meat. 6. Top with the fried egg, gochujang, and drizzle with soy sauce and sesame oil. 7. Mix everything together before serving.

name: Tteokbokki
instructions:
1. Boil water in a pot and add the Tteokbokki to the boiling water. 
2. Cook for around 10-15 minutes or until soft. 
3. Drain the Tteokbokki and set aside. 
4. In a separate pan, add gochujang (Korean chili paste) and sugar. Cook on low heat until it forms a sauce. 
5. Add the cooked Tteokbokki to the sauce and stir until well-coated. 
6. Serve hot and enjoy!

name: Japchae
instructions: 1. Soak the sweet potato noodles in hot water for about 30 minutes, until softened. Drain and cut into manageable lengths. 2. In a large pan, heat sesame oil and stir-fry the beef slices until browned. Remove and set aside. 3. In the same pan, add the onions, carrots, and mushrooms; stir-fry until soft. 4. Add the spinach and cook until wilted. 5. Return the beef to the pan, add the noodles, soy sauce, sugar, and mix everything thoroughly. 6. Add green onions last and stir well before serving.

name: Manakish
instructions: 1. In a large bowl, mix flour, yeast, and salt together. Add water gradually and knead to form a dough. Let it rise for about an hour. 2. Once risen, divide the dough into smaller balls and roll out into flat circles. 3. Mix olive oil with zaatar and spread on the rolled dough. 4. Sprinkle cheese, diced tomatoes, olives, and fresh mint on top. 5. Bake in a preheated oven at 220°C (428°F) for about 10-15 minutes or until golden brown. 6. Serve warm with additional olives and mint if desired.

name: Chicken Shawarma
instructions:
1. Marinate the chicken with yogurt, garlic, olive oil, cumin, paprika, turmeric, coriander, salt, and pepper for several hours (or overnight).
2. Grill or cook the marinated chicken until fully cooked and slightly charred.
3. Slice the cooked chicken thinly.
4. Serve the chicken in pita bread topped with lettuce, tomato, onion, tahini, and pickles.
5. Enjoy your Chicken Shawarma!

name: Hummus with Pita
instructions: 1. In a food processor, combine the chickpeas, tahini, olive oil, lemon juice, and garlic. 2. Blend until smooth, adding water as needed to reach desired consistency. 3. Serve in a bowl, drizzle with more olive oil, and garnish if desired. 4. Cut pita bread into triangles and serve alongside the hummus.

name: Kibbeh
instructions: 1. Mix the ground meat with bulgur, spices, and onions to form a dough. 2. Shape it into balls or patties. 3. Fry or bake until golden brown. 4. Serve hot with yogurt or salad.

name: Labneh with Olive Oil
instructions:
1. Spread Labneh on a plate.
2. Drizzle olive oil on top.
3. Serve with a sprinkle of salt and pepper if desired.

name: Falafel
instructions: 1. Soak chickpeas overnight in water. 2. Drain them and combine with parsley, onion, garlic, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper in a food processor. 3. Blend until a coarse mixture is formed. 4. Add flour and baking powder, mixing well. 5. Shape into balls or patties. 6. Heat oil in a frying pan and fry the falafel until golden brown. 7. Serve hot with tahini sauce.

name: Tacos al Pastor
instructions:
1. Marinate the pork shoulder in a mixture of pineapple juice, garlic, onion, chili powder, cumin, and oregano for at least 4 hours or overnight.
2. Cook the marinated pork shoulder on a grill or in a skillet until cooked through and slightly charred, then slice thinly.
3. Warm the corn tortillas on the grill or in a pan.
4. Assemble the tacos by placing some sliced pork on each tortilla, topping with chopped onions, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime juice.
5. Serve hot and enjoy your Tacos al Pastor!

name: Chicken Enchiladas
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a pan, heat olive oil and sauté chopped onions and garlic until translucent.
3. Add shredded chicken and cumin; mix well.
4. In a separate bowl, mix enchilada sauce with a little water to thin it out.
5. Take a corn tortilla, fill it with the chicken mixture, roll it up, and place it seam-side down in a baking dish.
6. Repeat for all tortillas, then pour the enchilada sauce over the top and sprinkle with cheese.
7. Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, until the cheese is bubbly and golden.
8. Serve hot with additional salsa on the side.

name: Pozole
instructions: 1. In a large pot, place the chicken and cover with water. 2. Add garlic, onion, and seasonings (cumin, oregano, salt, and black pepper). 3. Bring to a boil, then reduce to simmer for about 1 hour until chicken is cooked through. 4. Remove the chicken, shred, and set aside. 5. Stir in pozole and let simmer for another 30 minutes. 6. Serve in bowls, topped with cabbage, radishes, avocado, and a squeeze of lime juice.

name: Lamb Tagine with Prunes
instructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. 
2. Add chopped onion and garlic, sauté until translucent. 
3. Stir in the ginger, cumin, cinnamon, salt, and pepper, and cook for 2 minutes. 
4. Add the lamb, browning on all sides. 
5. Pour in the chicken broth and bring to a simmer. 
6. Add prunes, cover, and cook for 1.5 to 2 hours, until the lamb is tender. 
7. Stir in the almonds just before serving. 
8. Serve with couscous or bread.

name: Shakshouka
instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. 2. Add chopped onions and bell pepper, and sauté until soft. 3. Add minced garlic, cumin, and smoked paprika, and cook for another minute. 4. Stir in the chopped tomatoes and season with salt and pepper. 5. Let the mixture simmer until the tomatoes break down. 6. Make small wells in the sauce and crack the eggs into each well. 7. Cover the skillet and cook until the eggs are just set. 8. Serve hot with crusty bread.

name: Aloo Paratha
instructions: 1. Boil and mash the potatoes. 2. In a bowl, mix whole wheat flour, salt, and water to make a soft dough. 3. Divide the dough into equal balls. 4. Roll out each ball into a flat circle. 5. Place a spoonful of mashed potatoes mixed with salt and cumin in the center. 6. Fold the dough around the filling, sealing it well. 7. Roll out gently to form a flat paratha. 8. Heat oil in a pan and cook the paratha until both sides are golden brown. 9. Serve hot with yogurt or pickle.

name: Samosa
instructions: 1. Boil the potatoes and mash them. 2. In a pan, heat oil, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. 3. Add peas, chilies and mashed potatoes. 4. Mix in salt, and chopped coriander leaves. 5. Prepare the dough by mixing flour, water and salt. 6. Roll out the dough and cut into circles. 7. Fill the dough with the potato mixture and shape into triangles. 8. Deep fry until golden brown. 9. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Rogan Josh
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan and sauté onions until golden brown. 2. Add garlic, ginger, and cumin seeds; sauté for a minute. 3. Add lamb meat and cook until browned. 4. Stir in yogurt and tomatoes; mix well. 5. Add cardamom, turmeric, and red chili. 6. Simmer for 40-50 minutes on low heat until lamb is tender. 7. Adjust seasoning and serve hot with rice or naan.

name: Samosa Chaat
instructions:
1. Prepare the samosas by frying or baking them until golden brown.
2. In a bowl, mash the chickpeas and mix with chopped onions, diced tomatoes, and chaat masala.
3. Place the samosas on a serving dish and top with the chickpea mixture.
4. Drizzle with yogurt and garnish with chopped cilantro.
5. Serve immediately and enjoy your Samosa Chaat!

name: Anda Paratha
instructions: 1. In a large bowl, combine 2 cups of whole wheat flour, 0.5 teaspoon of salt, and 2 beaten eggs. Mix well. 2. Gradually add 0.5 cups of water to form a dough and knead until smooth. 3. Divide the dough into equal portions and roll them into flat circles. 4. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a skillet over medium heat. 5. Cook each flatbread for 2-3 minutes on each side or until golden brown. 6. Serve hot with a side of chutney or yogurt.

name: Nihari
instructions: 1. Prepare the dough for naan and let it rest. 2. In a large pot, heat oil over medium heat. 3. Add meat, ginger, garlic, and spices, cooking until the meat is browned. 4. Pour in enough water to cover, and let it simmer on low heat for several hours. 5. During the last hour, add the prepared dough to thicken the gravy. 6. Serve hot with naan or rice and enjoy!

name: Syrniki
instructions: 1. In a bowl, mix the cottage cheese, egg, sugar, vanilla extract, and salt until smooth. 2. Gradually add the flour and mix until a dough forms. 3. Form small patties from the dough. 4. Heat a pan over medium heat with a little oil or butter. 5. Fry the patties until golden brown on both sides, about 4-5 minutes each side. 6. Serve hot with sour cream or jam.

name: Pirozhki
instructions: 1. Prepare the dough by mixing flour, yeast, and water until smooth. 2. Roll the dough into small balls and let them rest. 3. Fill each dough ball with your chosen filling. 4. Seal the dough around the filling and shape into small pies. 5. Fry or bake until golden brown. 6. Serve warm.

name: Kasha
instructions: 1. Rinse the kasha under cold water until the water runs clear. 2. In a pot, bring water to a boil. Add kasha, salt, and reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for about 15-20 minutes until the kasha is tender and the water is absorbed. 3. Add butter and stir until melted. 4. Serve warm, optionally with milk.

name: Olivier Salad
instructions: 1. Boil potatoes and carrots until soft, then cool and dice them. 2. Dice the pickles, cooked chicken, and boiled eggs. 3. In a large bowl, combine the diced potatoes, carrots, peas, pickles, chicken, and eggs. 4. Add mayonnaise, salt, and black pepper to taste, and mix well. 5. Refrigerate for at least an hour before serving.

name: Bunny Chow
instructions: 1. Take a loaf of bread and hollow out the center to form a bowl. 2. Prepare curry using the curry powder and mixed vegetables. 3. Fill the hollowed-out bread with the curry. 4. Serve immediately with the bread exterior as a side.

name: Vetkoek
instructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar, salt, and yeast. Mix well.
2. Gradually add warm water and knead the dough until smooth.
3. Cover and let it rise for about an hour or until doubled in size.
4. Once risen, shape the dough into small balls or ovals.
5. Heat oil in a deep pan and fry the dough balls until golden brown on both sides.
6. Remove and drain on paper towels. Serve warm.

name: Fish Ambul Thiyal
instructions: 1. Cut the fish into thick pieces. 2. In a bowl, mix the tamarind, turmeric powder, black pepper, garlic, ginger, and salt. 3. Coat the fish pieces with this mixture and let it marinate for at least 30 minutes. 4. In a pot, add water and bring it to a boil. 5. Once boiling, add the marinated fish and the cinnamon stick. 6. Reduce the heat and let it simmer until the fish is cooked through. 7. Add curry leaves just before serving.

name: Kottu Roti
ingredients: [{"id": 152, "name": "roti flour", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 235, "name": "Mixed Vegetables", "quantity": 150.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 240, "name": "eggs", "quantity": 3.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 44, "name": "chicken breast", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 163, "name": "soy sauce", "quantity": 50.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 443, "name": "Chili flakes", "quantity": 5.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 444, "name": "Kottu Roti", "quantity": 150.0, "serving_unit": "g"}]
instructions: 1. Heat a large pan and add butter. 2. Add chopped mixed vegetables and diced chicken. Cook until chicken is done. 3. Beat the eggs separately and add to the pan, mixing well. 4. Add the previously prepared roti and stir well to combine all ingredients. 5. Add soy sauce and chili flakes, mixing consistently until everything is well combined and heated through. Serve hot.

name: Satay Skewers
ingredients: [{"id": 1, "name": "chicken breast", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 2, "name": "peanut butter", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 3, "name": "soy sauce", "quantity": 50.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 4, "name": "coconut milk", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 5, "name": "garlic", "quantity": 3.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 6, "name": "ginger", "quantity": 20.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 7, "name": "lime juice", "quantity": 30.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 8, "name": "brown sugar", "quantity": 25.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 9, "name": "skewers", "quantity": 10.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}]
instructions:
1. Cut chicken breast into bite-sized pieces and marinate in a mixture of soy sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, minced garlic, and ginger for at least 30 minutes.
2. Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers.
3. Grill the skewers over medium heat for about 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally until fully cooked.
4. Meanwhile, prepare the peanut sauce by mixing peanut butter, coconut milk, and a little soy sauce until smooth.
5. Serve grilled skewers with the peanut sauce on the side.

name: Jok (Rice Porridge)
instructions:
1. Rinse the rice thoroughly until the water runs clear. 
2. Combine rice and water in a pot, bring to a boil, then lower heat to simmer. 
3. Stir occasionally and cook until the rice is soft and the porridge is creamy. 
4. In a separate pan, cook chicken breast with ginger and garlic until done. 
5. Shred the chicken and set aside. 
6. Stir in fish sauce, salt, and pepper into the porridge. 
7. Serve hot, garnished with shredded chicken and green onions on top.

name: Lagman Noodles
ingredients: [{"id": 1, "name": "Lagman Noodles", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 242, "name": "beef stock", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 281, "name": "ground beef", "quantity": 300.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 416, "name": "chopped onions", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 32, "name": "carrot", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 310, "name": "bell pepper", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 178, "name": "tomato", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 75, "name": "garlic", "quantity": 5.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 163, "name": "soy sauce", "quantity": 50.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 188, "name": "water", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 158, "name": "salt", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 18, "name": "black pepper", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 237, "name": "vegetable oil", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}]
instructions:
1. Cook the Lagman noodles according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
2. In a large pan or wok, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat.
3. Add the chopped onions and fry until golden brown.
4. Add ground beef and cook until browned.
5. Stir in sliced bell pepper, carrot, and tomatoes. Cook for about 5 minutes until they soften.
6. Add minced garlic and soy sauce, continuing to stir for another minute.
7. Pour in beef stock and water, season with salt and black pepper, then let simmer for 10 minutes.
8. Combine the cooked noodles with the beef and vegetable mixture. Stir well and serve hot.

name: Puff-Puff
instructions:
1. In a bowl, mix the flour, sugar, yeast, and salt. 
2. Gradually add water to the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth batter forms. 
3. Cover the bowl and allow the batter to rise for about 1 hour. 
4. Heat oil in a deep fryer or large pot.
5. Once the oil is hot, scoop small portions of the batter and fry until golden brown.
6. Remove and drain on paper towels. Serve warm!

name: Hausa Koko
instructions:
1. Soak 100g of finger millet in water for about 6-8 hours. Drain and rinse. 
2. Grind the soaked millet with 10g of ginger and water to form a smooth batter. 
3. Pour the batter into a pot and cook on medium heat while stirring continuously. 
4. Add 50g of sugar to taste and cook until it thickens to a porridge-like consistency. 
5. Serve warm, optionally with milk or more sugar.

name: Waakye
instructions:
1. In a large pot, combine the rice and black-eyed peas with plenty of water.
2. Add the Waakye leaves to the pot to give the dish its unique color.
3. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer until the rice and peas are tender (about 20-30 minutes).
4. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking and ensure even cooking.
5. Add salt to taste before serving.
6. Serve hot with your choice of protein, fried plantains, or spicy sauces.

name: Chicken Schnitzel Sandwich
instructions: 1. Preheat the oil in a frying pan over medium heat. 2. Flatten the chicken breasts to even thickness. 3. Dip the chicken in beaten egg, then coat with breadcrumbs. 4. Fry the chicken in hot oil until golden brown and cooked through. 5. Assemble sandwiches by placing a schnitzel on bread, adding lettuce and tomato, and spreading mayonnaise if desired. 6. Close the sandwich and serve hot.

name: Full English Breakfast
instructions: 1. Fry the bacon in a pan until crispy. 2. In the same pan, add the sausages and cook until browned. 3. Add the mushrooms and tomatoes, cooking until soft. 4. Fry the eggs to your preference in a little oil or butter. 5. Heat the baked beans in a small pan. 6. Serve everything on a plate with toast and enjoy!

name: Shepherd's Pie
instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F). 2. Cook the ground lamb in a pan over medium heat until browned, about 5-7 minutes. 3. Add chopped onions, carrots, peas, and garlic, and cook for another 5 minutes. 4. Stir in Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper. 5. In another pot, boil potatoes until tender, then mash with butter and milk. 6. Spread the lamb mixture in an ovenproof dish and top with mashed potatoes. 7. Bake in the oven for 25-30 minutes until golden on top.

name: Beef Wellington
ingredients: [{"id": 242, "name": "beef stock", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 87, "name": "ground beef", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 263, "name": "puff pastry", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 113, "name": "mushrooms", "quantity": 300.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 284, "name": "prosciutto", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 463, "name": "egg yolk", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 437, "name": "mustard", "quantity": 20.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 158, "name": "salt", "quantity": 10.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 18, "name": "black pepper", "quantity": 5.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 175, "name": "thyme", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 119, "name": "olive oil", "quantity": 30.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}]
instructions: 1. Preheat your oven to 200°C (400°F). 2. Sear the beef fillet in olive oil on all sides until browned. 3. Remove from heat and let it cool. 4. In a pan, sauté chopped mushrooms and thyme until moisture evaporates. 5. Lay prosciutto slices on cling film, overlapping them slightly. 6. Spread mushroom mixture over prosciutto, then place the beef on top and roll tightly. 7. Chill in the fridge for 15 minutes. 8. Roll out puff pastry and wrap the beef, sealing edges with egg yolk. 9. Brush the pastry with egg yolk and score lightly. 10. Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden brown. 11. Rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.

name: Mapo Tofu
instructions:
1. Drain and cube the tofu. Boil in salted water for 2-3 minutes and set aside.
2. In a pan, heat soy sauce and sesame oil. Add minced garlic and ginger, and sauté until fragrant.
3. Add ground pork and doubanjiang, cook until the pork is browned.
4. Gently stir in the tofu and water, and simmer for 5 minutes.
5. Mix cornstarch with a little water to create a slurry, then add to the pan to thicken the sauce.
6. Garnish with chopped green onions and serve hot.

name: Mandazi
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Mix well. 2. Add the coconut milk and water to the dry ingredients, and mix to form a soft dough. 3. Cover the dough and let it rest for about 30 minutes. 4. Roll out the dough to about 1/2 inch thickness and cut into desired shapes (triangles or circles). 5. Heat oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat. 6. Fry the dough pieces until golden brown on both sides. 7. Remove and drain on paper towels. 8. Serve warm with tea or coffee.

name: Zanzibar Fish Curry
instructions:
1. Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. 
2. Add chopped onions, garlic, and ginger, then sauté until onions are translucent. 
3. Stir in the curry powder and cook for a minute. 
4. Add tomatoes and cook until they soften. 
5. Pour in the coconut milk, bring to a simmer. 
6. Add fish fillets and simmer until cooked through. 
7. Season with salt, pepper, and lime juice before serving.

name: Quiche Lorraine
instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). 2. In a mixing bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and nutmeg. 3. Stir in the cheese, cooked bacon, and flour until well combined. 4. In a pie dish, melt the butter and coat the bottom and sides. 5. Pour the mixture into the prepared pie dish. 6. Bake for 30-35 minutes or until golden and set. 7. Let cool slightly before slicing and serving.

name: Crêpes
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, combine the flour, sugar, and salt. 2. In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and milk. 3. Gradually add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients, whisking until smooth. 4. Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and melt a small amount of butter. 5. Pour a ladle of the batter into the skillet and swirl to coat the surface. 6. Cook for about 1-2 minutes until the edges are golden, then flip and cook for an additional minute. 7. Repeat with the remaining batter, adding butter to the skillet as needed. 8. Serve with toppings of your choice.

name: Coq au Vin
instructions: 1. In a large pot, heat olive oil and butter over medium heat. 2. Add the bacon and cook until crispy, then remove and set aside. 3. Season the chicken with salt and black pepper, then brown it in the same pot. 4. Add the chopped onion, carrots, and garlic. Cook until the vegetables are softened. 5. Pour in the red wine and add thyme and bay leaves. Bring to a simmer. 6. Return the chicken and bacon to the pot. Cover and cook for about 1-1.5 hours, until the chicken is tender. 7. In the last 30 minutes, add the mushrooms. 8. Adjust seasoning, if necessary, and serve hot with fresh bread or potatoes.

name: Ratatouille
instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). 2. Dice the eggplant, zucchini, bell pepper, tomatoes, onion, and garlic. 3. In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. 4. Add the onion and garlic, cooking until soft. 5. Stir in the eggplant and bell pepper, cooking for about 5 minutes. 6. Add zucchini and tomatoes, stirring to combine. 7. Season with herbs de Provence, salt, and pepper. 8. Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and cover with foil. 9. Bake for 45 minutes, uncovering for the last 15 minutes.

name: Souvlaki Wrap
ingredients: [{"id": 343, "name": "Souvlaki Wrap", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 43, "name": "chicken", "quantity": 250.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 119, "name": "olive oil", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 75, "name": "garlic", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 61, "name": "dried oregano", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 158, "name": "salt", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 18, "name": "black pepper", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 369, "name": "pita bread", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 178, "name": "tomato", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 146, "name": "red onion", "quantity": 0.5, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 57, "name": "cucumber", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 478, "name": "tzatziki sauce", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}]
instructions: 1. Marinate the chicken with olive oil, garlic, oregano, salt, and black pepper for at least 15 minutes. 2. Grill the chicken until cooked through and slightly charred. 3. Warm the pita bread on a grill or in a pan. 4. Slice the grilled chicken and place it into the warm pita. 5. Top with chopped tomatoes, sliced red onion, and cucumber. 6. Drizzle tzatziki sauce over the top. 7. Wrap the pita around the filling and enjoy!

name: Dolmades
instructions: 1. Rinse the rice and soak it in water for 30 minutes. Drain and set aside. 2. In a pan, sauté the chopped onion until translucent. 3. In a bowl, mix the rice, ground meat, sautéed onion, herbs, and lemon juice together. 4. Lay a grape leaf flat, place a spoonful of the filling at the base, and roll it tightly, folding in the sides. 5. Place the rolled dolmades seam-side down in a pot. 6. Cover the dolmades with leftover grape leaves, add some water to cover, and simmer for about 40 minutes. 7. Serve warm with lemon wedges.

name: Bougatsa
instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F). 2. In a greased baking tray, layer 4 sheets of phyllo pastry, brushing each with melted butter. 3. Spread the filling evenly over the phyllo. 4. Cover the filling with the remaining phyllo sheets, again brushing each with butter. 5. Cut into squares and bake for 30-40 minutes or until golden. 6. Dust with powdered sugar and cinnamon before serving.

name: Cornetti
instructions: To make cornetti, start by preparing the dough with flour, sugar, yeast, milk, butter, and a pinch of salt. Knead until smooth and allow to rise. Roll out the dough and cut into triangles, then fill with jam or chocolate, roll them up, and let them rise again before baking until golden.

name: Margherita Pizza
instructions: Preheat the oven to 220°C (428°F). Roll out the pizza dough into a circular shape. Spread tomato sauce evenly over the base. Tear fresh mozzarella and distribute it over the sauce. Scatter fresh basil leaves on top and drizzle with olive oil. Sprinkle with salt. Bake for 10-15 minutes until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbly. Allow to cool slightly before slicing and serving.

name: Hotteok (Sweet Pancakes)
instructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar, and yeast. 
2. In another bowl, mix milk, water, and vegetable oil. 
3. Gradually add the liquid ingredients to the dry ingredients, mixing until dough forms. 
4. Knead for a few minutes until smooth and let it rise for 30 minutes. 
5. Divide the dough into small balls and flatten them slightly. 
6. In the center of each dough ball, place chopped nuts and a sprinkle of cinnamon. 
7. Fold the dough over the filling and seal well. 
8. Heat a pan and place the pancakes, cooking until golden brown on both sides. 
9. Serve warm and enjoy!

name: Elote (Street Corn)
instructions: 1. Preheat your grill to medium-high heat. 2. Grill the corn for about 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally until charred. 3. In a bowl, mix the mayonnaise, lime juice, chili powder, and a pinch of salt until smooth. 4. Once the corn is done, brush it with melted butter. 5. Spread the mayonnaise mixture over the corn. 6. Sprinkle the cotija cheese and fresh cilantro on top. 7. Serve with extra lime wedges on the side for squeezing over the corn.

name: Huevos Rancheros
instructions:
1. In a skillet, heat a bit of oil over medium heat. Add chopped onions and jalapeños and sauté until soft. 
2. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they start to break down. Season with salt and pepper. 
3. In another pan, fry the eggs sunny-side up until the whites are set but yolks remain runny. 
4. Warm the tortillas in a separate skillet or microwave. 
5. To assemble, place tortillas on plates, top with the tomato-jalapeño mixture, then the fried eggs. 
6. Garnish with avocado slices, cilantro, and cheese. Serve immediately.

name: Chicken Tagine
instructions:
1. In a tagine or heavy pot, heat some oil and add chicken pieces. Brown the chicken on all sides and then remove it.
2. In the same pot, add chopped onions, garlic, and ginger, and sauté until fragrant.
3. Return the chicken to the pot. Add carrots, preserved lemons (rinsed and chopped), saffron, turmeric, cumin, and cinnamon.
4. Add water or chicken broth to cover the chicken. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cover.
5. Let it simmer for about 45 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
6. Serve with olives and fresh herbs, accompanied by couscous or bread.

name: Baghir (Moroccan Pancakes)
instructions: 1. In a bowl, mix the all-purpose flour, cornmeal, baking powder, sugar, yeast, and salt. 2. Gradually stir in the milk and water to form a smooth batter. 3. Cover the bowl and let it rest for 30 minutes until bubbles form. 4. Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Pour small amounts of batter to make pancakes and cook until holes form on the surface. 5. Flip and cook for another minute until golden. Enjoy warm with honey or syrup!

name: Couscous with Vegetables
instructions: 1. Prepare the couscous by bringing water to a boil and adding salt. 2. Stir in the couscous, cover, and remove from heat. 3. Let it sit for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork. 4. In a separate pan, heat olive oil over medium heat. 5. Add minced garlic and sauté for a minute. 6. Add the mixed vegetables and cook until tender. 7. Season with cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. 8. Combine the vegetables with the couscous and mix well. 9. Serve warm, garnished with additional herbs if desired.

name: Briouats
instructions: 1. In a pan, heat a little bit of oil and sauté chopped onions until translucent. 2. Add minced garlic and ground meat, cooking until browned. 3. Season with salt, black pepper, cinnamon, and chopped parsley to taste. 4. Let the filling cool. 5. Lay out a sheet of phyllo pastry and cut it into strips. 6. Place a spoonful of the meat mixture at one end and fold the pastry over to form a triangle shape, continuing to fold until sealed. 7. Fry the briouats in hot oil until golden brown, then drain on paper towels. 8. Serve hot as a snack.

name: Butter Chicken
ingredients: [{"id": 405, "name": "Butter Chicken", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 43, "name": "chicken", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 27, "name": "butter", "quantity": 50.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 411, "name": "yogurt", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 121, "name": "onion", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 75, "name": "garlic", "quantity": 4.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 77, "name": "ginger", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 178, "name": "tomato", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 502, "name": "extra cream", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 501, "name": "butter chicken spices", "quantity": 10.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 158, "name": "salt", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 18, "name": "black pepper", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 54, "name": "coriander powder", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}]
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken in yogurt, spices, salt, and ginger-garlic paste for at least 1 hour. 2. In a pan, melt butter and sauté onions until golden brown. 3. Add marinated chicken and cook until it starts to brown. 4. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. 5. Stir in cream and simmer for 15 minutes. 6. Adjust seasoning and garnish with coriander leaves. 7. Serve hot with naan or rice.

name: Poha
instructions:
1. Rinse the rice flakes in water and set aside for 10 minutes until soft.
2. In a pan, heat oil over medium heat. Add mustard seeds and allow them to crackle.
3. Add curry leaves and chopped green chilies; sauté for a minute.
4. Add chopped onions and sauté until translucent.
5. Add diced potatoes and peas; cook until the potatoes are tender.
6. Stir in turmeric and salt, then add the soaked rice flakes. Mix well.
7. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
8. Garnish with chopped coriander and serve hot.

name: Rajma Chawal
ingredients: [{"id": 505, "name": "Rajma", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 148, "name": "rice", "quantity": 300.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 121, "name": "onion", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 178, "name": "tomato", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 77, "name": "ginger", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 75, "name": "garlic", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 406, "name": "green chili", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 58, "name": "cumin seeds", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 183, "name": "turmeric powder", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 231, "name": "red chili powder", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 74, "name": "garam masala", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 158, "name": "salt", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 53, "name": "coriander leaves", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}]
instructions: 1. Soak Rajma (kidney beans) overnight in water. 2. Boil the Rajma in fresh water until soft. 3. In a pan, heat oil and add cumin seeds. 4. Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown. 5. Add ginger, garlic, and green chili - sauté for another minute. 6. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. 7. Stir in turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt. 8. Add the boiled Rajma and a little water; let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. 9. Add garam masala and chopped coriander leaves before serving. 10. Serve hot with rice.

name: Chicken Karahi
ingredients: [{"id": 451, "name": "Chicken", "quantity": 1000.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 415, "name": "cooking oil", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 121, "name": "onion", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 178, "name": "tomato", "quantity": 300.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 413, "name": "green chilies", "quantity": 50.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 77, "name": "ginger", "quantity": 30.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 75, "name": "garlic", "quantity": 30.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 54, "name": "coriander powder", "quantity": 20.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 223, "name": "cumin powder", "quantity": 10.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 158, "name": "salt", "quantity": 10.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 231, "name": "red chili powder", "quantity": 15.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 74, "name": "garam masala", "quantity": 10.0, "serving_unit": "g"}]
instructions: 1. Heat the oil in a karahi. 2. Add chopped onions and sauté until they are golden brown. 3. Add ginger and garlic paste, and sauté for a minute. 4. Add chicken pieces and cook until the chicken is browned. 5. Add chopped tomatoes and green chilies, and cook until the tomatoes are soft. 6. Add coriander powder, cumin powder, salt, red chili powder, and garam masala. 7. Mix well and let it simmer until the chicken is fully cooked. 8. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with naan or rice.

name: Haleem
instructions: 1. Soak wheat overnight in water. 2. In a large pot, add soaked wheat, ground beef, and 8 cups of water. 3. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the wheat is soft and well cooked. 4. Add hareem spices and mix well. 5. Cook for an additional 30 minutes. 6. Blend the mixture until smooth. 7. Simmer for another 10-15 minutes. 8. Serve hot, garnished with fried onions, lemon, and fresh cilantro.

name: Blini
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, combine the flour, milk, eggs, salt, and sugar. Whisk until smooth. 2. Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add a little butter. 3. Pour a ladle of the batter onto the skillet and cook until bubbles form on the surface. Flip and cook the other side until golden brown. 4. Repeat with the remaining batter. Serve warm with your choice of toppings.

name: Mealie Pap
instructions: 1. In a medium saucepan, bring water to a boil. 2. Gradually add Mealie Pap to the boiling water while stirring continuously to avoid lumps. 3. Reduce the heat to low and cook for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently until thickened. 4. Add salt and butter, mixing well until fully incorporated. 5. Serve hot, with your choice of accompaniments.

name: Vetkoek
ingredients: [{"id": 268, "name": "plain flour", "quantity": 250.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 393, "name": "active dry yeast", "quantity": 7.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 158, "name": "salt", "quantity": 5.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 256, "name": "brown sugar", "quantity": 30.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 188, "name": "water", "quantity": 250.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 415, "name": "cooking oil", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 459, "name": "minced beef", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 121, "name": "onion", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 325, "name": "cumin", "quantity": 5.0, "serving_unit": "g"}]
instructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the flour, yeast, salt, and sugar. 
2. Gradually add water and mix until a soft dough forms. 
3. Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until smooth. 
4. Allow the dough to rise for 1 hour in a warm place, covered with a cloth.
5. Meanwhile, cook the minced beef and onions with spices in a pan until the meat is browned. 
6. After the dough has risen, punch it down and divide it into balls. 
7. Flatten each ball and place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center. 
8. Fold the dough over the filling and seal it. 
9. Heat oil in a pan and deep fry the stuffed dough until golden brown on all sides. 
10. Drain on paper towels and serve warm.

name: Cape Malay Curry
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pot and sauté onions until translucent. 2. Add garlic, ginger, and chicken, cook until browned. 3. Stir in curry powder and cook for a minute. 4. Add tomatoes, coconut milk, and bring to a simmer. 5. Add potatoes and carrots, simmer until cooked through. 6. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Dosa
instructions: 1. Soak rice and urad dal in water for at least 4 hours. 2. Grind the soaked ingredients into a smooth batter. 3. Ferment the batter overnight in a warm place. 4. Heat a flat skillet and pour a ladle of batter, spreading it in a circular motion. 5. Cook until the bottom is golden brown, then flip it to cook the other side. 6. Serve hot with chutney and sambar.

name: Medu Vada
instructions: 1. Soak the urad dal for at least 4-5 hours or overnight. Drain and grind it to a smooth paste. 2. Transfer the ground dal paste to a mixing bowl. 3. Add chopped onions, green chilies, ginger, curry leaves, and salt to the dal paste and mix well. 4. Heat oil in a frying pan for deep frying. 5. Wet your hands, take a portion of the batter, shape it into a donut or vada, and gently drop it into the hot oil. 6. Fry until golden brown and crisp, then remove and drain on paper towels. 7. Serve hot with coconut chutney or sambar.

name: Banana Chips
instructions:
1. Peel the raw bananas and slice them thinly using a slicer or sharp knife.
2. Prepare a mixture of salt and turmeric in a bowl.
3. Heat oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat. 
4. Once the oil is hot, carefully add the sliced bananas in batches. Fry them until they are golden brown and crispy.
5. Remove the chips with a slotted spoon and drain them on paper towels. 
6. Sprinkle the salt-turmeric mixture over the hot banana chips.
7. Let them cool and store in an airtight container.

name: String Hoppers
instructions: 1. In a mixing bowl, combine rice flour and salt. 2. Gradually add coconut milk and enough water to form a smooth dough. 3. Fill a string hopper maker with the dough and press it out onto small round baskets. 4. Steam the baskets for about 10 minutes or until cooked through. 5. Serve hot with coconut sambol or curry.

name: Coconut Pancakes
instructions:
1. In a bowl, mix together the pancake flour, sugar, and salt. 
2. Add the coconut milk and water to the dry ingredients and stir until smooth. 
3. Fold in the shredded coconut. 
4. Heat coconut oil in a frying pan over medium heat. 
5. Pour a small amount of batter into the pan and cook until bubbles form on the surface. Flip and cook until golden brown. 
6. Repeat with the remaining batter. 
7. Serve warm with extra coconut or syrup if desired.

name: Chicken Curry
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. 2. Add chopped onions and sauté until they are soft and translucent. 3. Add minced garlic and sauté for another minute. 4. Stir in the curry powder and mix well. 5. Add the chicken pieces and cook until they are lightly browned. 6. Pour in the coconut milk and stir to combine. 7. Season with salt and black pepper, and add green chilies. 8. Cover and simmer for about 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through. 9. Adjust seasoning as necessary and serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Pad Thai
instructions:
1. Soak rice noodles in warm water for about 30 minutes. Drain and set aside.
2. In a large skillet or wok, heat some oil over medium heat. Add the shrimp and cook until pink.
3. Push shrimp to the side and crack the eggs into the pan. Scramble them and then mix with shrimp.
4. Add the soaked noodles, fish sauce, lime juice, and sugar. Stir-fry everything for 3-5 minutes until heated through.
5. Add bean sprouts and chopped green onions, mixing well for another minute.
6. Serve hot, garnished with peanuts and lime wedges on the side.

name: Tom Yum Soup
instructions: 1. In a pot, bring 4 cups of water to a boil. 2. Add the lemongrass, galangal, and lime leaves. Simmer for 5 minutes. 3. Add the mushrooms and cook for another 2 minutes. 4. Stir in the shrimp and cook until they turn pink. 5. Add the fish sauce, lime juice, and chili paste. 6. Garnish with cilantro and serve hot.

name: Green Curry Chicken
ingredients: [{"id": 1, "name": "chicken thighs", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 2, "name": "green curry paste", "quantity": 3.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 3, "name": "coconut milk", "quantity": 400.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 4, "name": "vegetable oil", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 5, "name": "bamboo shoots", "quantity": 150.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 6, "name": "bell peppers", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 7, "name": "Thai basil", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "cup"}, {"id": 8, "name": "fish sauce", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 9, "name": "sugar", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 10, "name": "lime leaves", "quantity": 4.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}]
instructions: 1. In a pan, heat vegetable oil over medium heat. 2. Add the green curry paste and sauté until fragrant. 3. Add chicken thighs and cook until browned. 4. Pour in coconut milk and stir. 5. Add bamboo shoots, bell peppers, fish sauce, sugar, and lime leaves. 6. Simmer for 20 minutes. 7. Add Thai basil before serving. 8. Serve with rice.

name: Massaman Curry
ingredients: [{"id": 552, "name": "Massaman Curry", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 43, "name": "chicken", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 322, "name": "coconut milk", "quantity": 400.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 324, "name": "potato", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 121, "name": "onion", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 32, "name": "carrot", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 357, "name": "bay leaf", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 127, "name": "peanuts", "quantity": 50.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 553, "name": "Massaman curry paste", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 417, "name": "chopped coriander leaves", "quantity": 30.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 158, "name": "salt", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 226, "name": "sugar", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}]
instructions: 1. In a large pot, heat some oil over medium heat. 2. Add the onions and cook until they are translucent. 3. Stir in the Massaman curry paste and cook for a minute until fragrant. 4. Add the chicken and cook until it's browned on all sides. 5. Pour in the coconut milk and add the bay leaf. 6. Add the potatoes and carrots, and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. 7. Cover and cook for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender. 8. Stir in the peanuts, coriander leaves, salt, and sugar. 9. Adjust seasoning as needed and serve hot with rice.

name: Non Bread with Honey
instructions:
1. Place the Non Bread on a plate. 
2. Drizzle the Honey over the Non Bread. 
3. Enjoy your meal!

name: Plov
instructions: 1. Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat. 2. Add chopped onions and sauté until golden. 3. Add the meat and cook until browned. 4. Stir in grated carrots and sauté for a few minutes. 5. Add the rice, salt, and pepper, stirring to mix well. 6. Pour in the water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for about 20 minutes. 7. Once the rice is cooked, fluff with a fork and serve hot.

name: Samsa
instructions: 1. In a bowl, mix flour, water, and a pinch of salt to form a dough. Knead it until smooth. Let it rest for 30 minutes. 2. In another bowl, mix minced beef, chopped onion, salt, black pepper, and caraway seeds. 3. Roll the dough into thin circles. Place a spoonful of the beef mixture in the center. Fold the dough over and seal the edges. 4. Preheat the oven to 200°C (390°F). 5. Brush the tops of the samsas with beaten egg. 6. Bake for about 30 minutes or until golden brown. 7. Serve hot as a delicious snack.

name: Semolina Porridge
instructions:
1. Bring water to a boil in a saucepan. 
2. Add semolina gradually while stirring to avoid lumps. 
3. Cook for about 5-7 minutes until it thickens, stirring continuously. 
4. Add salt and sugar to taste. 
5. Remove from heat and stir in milk. 
6. Serve warm, and you can top with fruit or nuts as desired.

name: Manti Dumplings
instructions: 1. Prepare the manti dough by combining flour, water, and a pinch of salt. Knead until smooth and let it rest. 2. For the filling, mix minced meat with chopped onions, garlic, salt, and spices. 3. Roll out the dough and cut it into squares. 4. Place a spoonful of the meat mixture in the center of each square and fold it into a dumpling shape. 5. Steam the dumplings for about 40 minutes. 6. Serve hot, topped with sour cream or a garlic sauce.

name: Jollof Rice
ingredients: [{"id": 1, "name": "long-grain parboiled rice", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "cup"}, {"id": 2, "name": "vegetable oil", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "cup"}, {"id": 3, "name": "onions", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 4, "name": "tomatoes", "quantity": 4.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 5, "name": "red bell peppers", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 6, "name": "garlic", "quantity": 3.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 7, "name": "ginger", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tbsp"}, {"id": 8, "name": "chicken stock", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "cup"}, {"id": 9, "name": "thyme", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 10, "name": "bay leaves", "quantity": 2.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 11, "name": "salt", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}, {"id": 12, "name": "pepper", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}]
instructions:
1. Rinse the rice in cold water until the water runs clear. Drain and set aside.
2. Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add sliced onions and sauté until translucent.
3. Blend tomatoes, red bell peppers, garlic, and ginger into a smooth paste. Add to the pot and cook for about 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Add chicken stock, thyme, bay leaves, salt, and pepper to the pot. Bring to a boil.
5. Stir in the rice and ensure it's well coated with the sauce. Cover and reduce heat to low. Cook for 20-30 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
6. Fluff the rice with a fork, remove bay leaves, and serve hot.

name: Egusi Soup
ingredients: [{"id": 563, "name": "Egusi", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 9, "name": "baby spinach", "quantity": 100.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 121, "name": "onion", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "nos"}, {"id": 400, "name": "meat (beef or goat)", "quantity": 500.0, "serving_unit": "g"}, {"id": 300, "name": "palm oil", "quantity": 200.0, "serving_unit": "ml"}, {"id": 275, "name": "seasoning (like Maggi or salt)", "quantity": 1.0, "serving_unit": "tsp"}]
instructions:
1. Begin by blending the Egusi seeds with water to form a thick paste.
2. Heat palm oil in a pot until hot, then add chopped onions and sauté until translucent.
3. Add the Egusi paste to the pot, stir well, and let it cook for about 10 minutes.
4. Add in the seasoned meat and any additional broth if necessary, and cook until the meat is tender.
5. Add in the spinach and cook for an additional 5-10 minutes until the spinach is wilted.
6. Adjust seasoning to taste and serve hot with fufu or rice.

name: Suya
instructions: 1. Cut the beef into thin slices and marinate with garlic, ginger, pepper, salt, and suya spice for at least 30 minutes. 2. Thread the marinated beef onto skewers. 3. Heat the groundnut oil in a grill pan over medium heat. 4. Grill the skewers for about 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally until the beef is cooked through. 5. Serve hot with sliced onions and pepper.
